description_long: This object depicts a stylized flower with two prominent, faceted purple blooms, each composed of angular, geometric petals. The larger bloom is upright and centrally positioned, while a smaller bloom is attached to the side, both emerging from a short, thick green stem. Two broad, dark green leaves extend outward at the base, providing a visual foundation and contrast to the purple petals. The petals are arranged in a somewhat radial pattern, overlapping and layered, giving the blooms a full, clustered appearance. The base of the flowers is supported by brown, angular shapes that resemble sepals or bracts. The overall form is abstract but clearly evokes the structure of a flowering plant commonly found in urban parks or landscaped areas.
description: A stylized flower with two faceted purple blooms, broad green leaves, and brown sepals, arranged in a clustered, geometric form.
description_view_0: A side view showing the vertical alignment of the main bloom and the layered arrangement of petals and leaves.
description_view_1: A frontal view highlighting both purple blooms, the broad green leaves, and the brown sepals at the base.
description_view_2: A side view from the opposite direction, emphasizing the stacked petals and the brown sepals supporting the blooms.
description_view_3: A rear-angled view showing the overlapping petals, the two blooms, and the spread of the green leaves.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial pattern around the center of each bloom, with overlapping and layered facets creating a clustered, whorled appearance. The arrangement lacks perfect symmetry but maintains a generally circular outline typical of many ornamental flowers.
